constraints.expression: consumer recomputes subject_hash via safeCanonicalize(committed-artifact) and rejects the record on mismatch
constraints.message: subject_hash MUST equal SHA-256 over safeCanonicalize(committed-artifact). The library does NOT recompute or compare; consumer-side reconciliation is required (INTEGRITY_DEFERRED).
constraints.evaluation_note: Content-addressed-hash reconciliation is consumer-side per ADR-010. The library's role is to ship the commitment envelope shape (commitment_id + commitment_type + subject_hash + optional onchain anchor fields); the consumer recomputes the hash over the committed artifact. The schema-level x-crypto-bearing flag forces explicit acceptDeferred opt-in; when opted in, the manifest entry surfaces this under reason: 'integrity_deferred'.

constraints.expression: (anchor_chain_id == null && anchor_tx_hash == null) || (anchor_chain_id != null && anchor_tx_hash != null)
constraints.message: anchor_chain_id and anchor_tx_hash are mutually presence-coupled: either both present (commitment is anchored on-chain) or both absent (commitment is local-only).
constraints.evaluation_note: Library-evaluated structural coupling. A CommitmentRoot record that carries anchor_chain_id without anchor_tx_hash (or vice versa) is structurally incomplete — the on-chain anchor is by-pair (chain id + transaction hash). The DSL expression encodes the biconditional via two-clause disjunction (both null OR both non-null) so cross-language runners (Go/Python/Rust) replicate the rule identically. The library evaluator runs the equivalent check in the cross-field validator; consumer-side workflows that omit the on-chain anchor entirely are valid (both fields absent).
